column1: What happens when the user wants a structure the Splits sheet does not list, like Push Pull Legs Push. The design principle: a custom split compiles down to the same day_structure format as a preset, is validated by these rules, and then flows through Session Templates, Weekly Volume, Prescription Logic and Safety Rules identically. No special cases downstream.

column3: A custom split is any ordered sequence of known day types (Push, Pull, Legs, Upper, Lower, Full, Focus, Cond) with length equal to the user's lifting days. It is stored as a day_structure string, exactly like the preset splits, so every downstream sheet works unchanged.
column4: Push Pull Legs Push is just 'Push|Pull|Legs|Push'. Nothing downstream needs to know it was custom.

column3: Every major muscle group must be hit directly at least once per week. If the sequence leaves a muscle at zero (Push Push Pull Push has no leg work), warn the user, and offer to convert one repeated day. If they insist, add the missing pattern as required slots on the closest day and note it on the program.
column4: The user can bias hard toward what they love, but nothing gets trained zero times without them being told.

column3: Repeated day types obey SCH03: at least 48 hours apart. 'Push|Push|Pull|Legs' is rearranged or rejected, 'Push|Pull|Legs|Push' is fine.
column4: You can press twice a week, just not on consecutive days.

column3: When a day type repeats, the weekly set budget for its muscles is split across the instances, not paid twice. The second instance also draws different exercises: the generator excludes slot picks already used that week and fills from different angles (flat becomes incline, barbell becomes dumbbell or cable).
column4: A second Push day means more variety at the same weekly budget, unless the muscle is a declared focal point, in which case the focal adjustment from Weekly Volume applies on top.

column3: Weekly Volume caps, all HARD_SAFETY rules, screening outcomes and the session budget apply to custom splits with no exceptions. A custom split changes which days exist, never how hard any muscle may be pushed.
column4: Custom means custom structure, not custom safety.

column3: If the user describes a split in words ('bro split', 'chest day Monday', 'arms twice a week'), the AI maps it to day types: a single muscle day maps to a Focus day for that muscle, 'arms twice' adds Biceps and Triceps as focal points on two days. If it cannot map the request, it offers the two nearest presets and explains the difference in one sentence each.
column4: Whatever the user asks for, they leave with a valid day_structure string. For common de-emphasis requests (e.g. 'legs once a week') use the Custom Split Resolution table for a deterministic day_structure rather than choosing freehand.

column3: Custom splits are offered to Intermediate and Advanced users. Beginners asking for one get it honoured where CS02 passes, with a one line note that the preset would serve them better, and never more push back than that.
column4: Guide, do not gatekeep. It is their program.
